title: Math essentials
explainContext: Core algebra and geometry formulas every student should know by heart.

lessons.title: Powers & roots
lessons.intro:
## Exponent rules as one system

Every exponent rule follows from a single idea: $a^n$ means *n copies of $a$ multiplied together*.

- Multiplying stacks copies: $a^m \cdot a^n = a^{m+n}$
- A power of a power multiplies the counts: $(a^m)^n = a^{mn}$
- Dividing removes copies: $a^m / a^n = a^{m-n}$ — which forces $a^0 = 1$ and $a^{-n} = 1/a^n$
- Roots are fractional counts: $\sqrt{a} = a^{1/2}$

If you remember the *idea*, you can rebuild any rule you forget.

cards.hint: Follow the quotient rule with $m = n$.
The 'difference of squares' — the middle terms cancel.

lessons.title: Geometry formulas
lessons.intro:
## Areas and volumes, organized

Most formulas here come in pairs: a **boundary** measure (circumference, surface) and a **content** measure (area, volume). Content formulas carry one more factor of length than boundary formulas — that's why circle *circumference* is $2\pi r$ (length¹) while *area* is $\pi r^2$ (length²).

Spot-check any formula you recall by counting dimensions: a volume must have three lengths multiplied, an area two.

cards.front: Area of a circle with radius $r$?
Circumference of a circle with radius $r$?
Area of a triangle with base $b$ and height $h$?
Pythagoras: the sides $a, b$ and hypotenuse $c$ of a right triangle satisfy …?
Volume of a cylinder with radius $r$ and height $h$?
Volume of a sphere with radius $r$?
Surface area of a sphere with radius $r$?
cards.hint: Exactly the derivative of the volume with respect to $r$.

cards.front: Volume of a cone with base radius $r$ and height $h$?
cards.hint: A third of the surrounding cylinder.
